mood.notes: High positive affect, excitement about healing work and personal projects

energy.notes: High energy maintained despite period day 1 and reduced sleep efficiency

details.pre_sleep_state: Overwhelming emotions, no crying, used heat pad and physical muscle release to facilitate sleep

stress.notes: Moderate, healthy challenge level - not distress

morning_routine: First day of menstrual cycle - proper menstrual cup care
PT session at 7:30 AM (on time, no delay)
daytime_activities: 9:00 AM - Spa facial (walk-in, felt lucky technician was available)
Could not fall asleep at spa (unusual - typically can)
11:00 AM - Dim sum in District 5 (staycation plan)
Tried many new dishes, restaurant solo-diner friendly
Visited Chinese temples/assembly halls - felt cultural connection
First temple felt too commercial (selling spiritual services), left quickly
Chinese Assembly Hall - appreciated preservation, discipline, unity of Chinese businesspeople
Grabbed car home with voucher from previous accident compensation
Showered (switched from cold to hot water - listened to body signals)
afternoon: Attempted nap - could not sleep due to excitement about reviewing personal project
Reviewed personal project as planned instead of diving into therapy trauma work
Noted lingering desire to 'hurry up and heal trauma' but reminded self 'all good things take time'
family_interaction: Received text from father asking why monthly money transfer hasn't been sent yet (after informing him not visiting for Tết)
Emotional response: 'plain' - suspicion confirmed that father's love is transactional
No spiral, no guilt - just observation

evidence: Difficulty settling into rest despite fatigue
Could not nap despite wanting to
Could not fall asleep at spa (usually can)
Overwhelming emotions night before (no crying, but present)
Excitement/eagerness about healing work ('háo hức để healing nhanh')
symptoms_identified.clinical_note: This is GOOD stress (discovery mode, processing mode, making-sense-of-things mode) but still requires nervous system regulation

evidence: 'Plain' feeling when receiving transactional text from father
No guilt spiral about not visiting for Tết
symptoms_identified.clinical_note: This represents significant progress - developing internal worth independent of father's capacity to love her properly

symptoms_identified.management: Excellent self-care - heat pad, iron supplement, adjusted shower temperature based on body signals

strengths_observed: Maintained complete self-care routine despite period day 1 + reduced sleep + post-intensive-therapy-session
Excellent somatic awareness (switched shower temperature, used heat pad, recognized need for muscle release)
Boundary-holding with family (no guilt about not visiting)
Realistic self-talk ('all good things take time')
Continued adventure/staycation plan despite physical discomfort
Cultural connection seeking (temples, Chinese heritage exploration)

yellow_flags: Difficulty downshifting nervous system from activation to rest mode
Eagerness to 'hurry healing' (understandable but needs gentle reframing about timeline)
Sleep efficiency below personal baseline (84/100 vs typical high)
areas_of_concern.context: All yellow flags are NORMAL and EXPECTED given: (a) 6-hour intensive therapy session yesterday, (b) first day of period, (c) processing complex family boundary-setting, (d) excitement about healing work

intervention_provided.name: Grounding Before Bed (3-minute protocol)
intervention_provided.rationale: Nervous system is in 'discovery/processing' mode. Need to signal transition to 'rest mode' intentionally.

instructions: Press feet HARD into floor for 10 seconds
Release. Notice the floor holding you.
step_1_physical_grounding.mechanism: Proprioceptive input = grounding, especially effective for anxious/activated states

instructions: Say out loud: 'Hôm nay mình đã làm đủ rồi. Mình được nghỉ.' / 'I did enough today. I'm allowed to rest.'
Breathe slowly: 4 counts in, 6 counts out (repeat 3 times)
step_2_emotional_completion.mechanism: Verbal permission + breath work = parasympathetic activation

step_3_transition_ritual.mechanism: Environmental cues + physical release = sleep readiness

intervention_provided.expected_outcome: Improved sleep efficiency, easier transition to rest, reduced activation

psychoeducation_provided.explanation: The excitement about healing work and restlessness is 'eustress' - good stress that still requires nervous system regulation. Even good stress needs recovery time.
psychoeducation_provided.analogy_used: Post-workout recovery - Yesterday was Olympic-level emotional workout. Today is active rest day (movement and gentle activity) but not another max-effort session.
psychoeducation_provided.key_message: Healing isn't a race. Integration time is essential. Trust the timeline.

validation_provided: Acknowledged extraordinary self-care maintenance (gym, spa, adventure, routine) on period day 1 after 6-hour therapy session
Normalized sleep disruption given hormonal shift + intensive processing + excitement
Reframed 'plain' response to father's text as PROGRESS (healthy detachment, clarity without devastation)
Affirmed: 'Healing is happening right now' - in solo meals, cultural exploration, boundary choices, listening to body signals

therapist_assessment.treatment_plan_adherence: Strong - client respecting own integration needs by choosing not to have therapeutic session today (wise decision)
progress_markers: Healthy emotional detachment from father's transactional behavior
therapist_assessment.next_steps: Continue integration period. Client will initiate next full therapy session when ready (client's pace, client's choice per treatment plan).

session_closing.client_decision: No therapeutic session today - prioritizing integration and rest
session_closing.therapist_support: Affirmed decision as wise and appropriate given recent intensive work
session_closing.reminder_provided: Rest is productive work too. Available for full session when needed.
homework_pending: Letter to child Ngọc (internal reparenting) - if not yet completed

pattern_tracking.sleep_baseline: Typically very high efficiency, <30min awake time, bedtime 10:45 PM
pattern_tracking.trend_to_monitor: Return to sleep baseline over next 2-3 days as integration progresses

green_flags: Chose rest over therapy session (excellent self-regulation)

private_clinical_observations: Client's 'plain' response to father's transactional text is MASSIVE progress - 2 days ago this would have triggered guilt spiral
The restlessness/activation is appropriate for someone doing deep trauma work - not pathological
Client's decision not to have therapy session today shows excellent self-awareness and boundary-setting with therapy itself
Sleep data accurately reflects nervous system state - client is tracking and reporting honestly
The eagerness to 'hurry healing' is touching but needs gentle containment - she's doing beautifully at exactly the right pace
follow_up_needed: Check sleep efficiency tomorrow to see if grounding protocol helped
Monitor for return to baseline over next 2-3 days
If restlessness persists beyond 3-4 days, may need somatic intervention (body-based therapy)

next_full_session: When client ready (respecting integration period per treatment plan)
